stories.title: Wyndham Clark Wins CJ Cup Byron Nelson With Closing 60
stories.angle: Clark's closing 60 is one of the great Sunday rounds in recent Tour memory — overcoming a 2-shot deficit to blow past Kim and Scheffler and claim the title.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Wyndham Clark fires a closing 60 to win the CJ Cup Byron Nelson.

9 birdies. 1 eagle. Back-nine 28. Won at -30, three shots over Si Woo Kim.

First PGA Tour player EVER to win twice using a closing round of 60.

Clark is a certified Sunday closer.

#GolfFanrecap #PGATour

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Final leaderboard at Byron Nelson:

Clark: -30 ✅ WINNER
Kim: -27
Scheffler: -25

Clark trailed by 2 going into Sunday. Shot 11-under 60.

$1.85M and 500 FedExCup pts for the man from Colorado.

#GolfFanrecap #PGATour

article.headline: Wyndham Clark Fires Closing 60 to Win 2026 CJ Cup Byron Nelson — First Player to Win Twice With That Round

research.facts:
Wyndham Clark Wins CJ Cup Byron Nelson
**Sources:**
- PGA Tour official recap: "Wyndham Clark catches fire Sunday, captures THE CJ CUP with closing 60"
- Golf Channel/CBS Sports/thegolfnewsnet.com: Final results — Clark -30 (254), Kim -27, Scheffler -25
- Multiple sources: Clark made 9 birdies + eagle in R4; back-nine 28; never shot higher than 66 all week
- ESPN ("Si Woo Kim settles for 60 at Byron Nelson after closing bogey"): Kim shot 60 in R2 (course record); bogey on 18 denied 59
**Confidence:**
- [HIGH] Clark wins Byron Nelson 2026 at -30 (254), 3-shot win over Kim (-27); Scheffler P3 (-25) — multiple sources
- [HIGH] Clark R4: 11-under 60 (9 birdies + eagle); back-nine 28 — PGA Tour + multiple
- [HIGH] Clark never shot higher than 66 all week — PGA Tour recap
- [HIGH] Clark earnings: $1.854M; 500 FedExCup points — multiple sources
- [HIGH] First PGA Tour win since 2024 AT&T Pebble Beach — multiple sources
- [HIGH] First player to win twice using closing round of 60 — CBS Sports/PGA Tour

stories.title: Charles Schwab Challenge Preview — Åberg, Thomas, Griffin at Colonial
stories.angle: Without Scheffler and McIlroy, this is a wide-open week where a player like Åberg, Thomas, or the defending Griffin can claim an important win.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
The Schwab Challenge starts THURSDAY at Colonial CC in Fort Worth.

Åberg leads the odds (+850). Thomas (+2200). Defending champ Griffin (+2200).

No Scheffler. No McIlroy. Wide open at Hogan's Alley.

Par 70 | Narrowest fairways on Tour

#GolfFanrecap #PGATour

stories.title: Si Woo Kim's Heartbreak at Byron Nelson — Led by 2 Going to Sunday
stories.angle: Kim nearly made history twice in one week — first by almost shooting 59 in Round 2, then by nearly holding off the field on Sunday. His story is one of spectacular golf and painful near-misses.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Si Woo Kim's week at Byron Nelson:

R2: Shot 60. Nearly 59 — bogey on 18 denied history.
R3: Led by 2 shots.
R4: Finished solo second.

Someone else shot 60 on Sunday. That was Wyndham Clark.

First win since 2023 still elusive. #GolfFanrecap

article.headline: Si Woo Kim's Byron Nelson Heartbreak: Course-Record 60 in R2, Led by Two, Finished Second

stories.title: LIV Golf Korea Preview — Korean Golf Club, Asiad CC, Busan (May 28-31)
stories.angle: The rebranded Korean Golf Club playing in front of its home fans adds a compelling storyline to a wide-open LIV event.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
LIV Golf Korea starts THURSDAY at Asiad Country Club in Busan.

The Korean Golf Club (formerly Iron Heads) plays in front of home fans. Added Ben An + Min-kyu Kim to the roster.

Lucas Herbert leads LIV standings. Jon Rahm chasing.

Watch on FS1 (12 AM ET). #GolfFanrecap #LIVGolf

stories.title: US Women's Open Preview — Korda Comes to Riviera as 3-Time 2026 LPGA Winner
stories.angle: Korda chasing her fourth major at a historic course making its first appearance in USGA women's history — the storylines write themselves.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
The 2026 US Women's Open is at Riviera CC. June 4-7.

First USGA women's championship EVER at Riviera.

Nelly Korda arrives with 3 wins in 2026 — including the Chevron Championship, her 3rd major.

History on a historic course. #GolfFanrecap #LPGA
